name: Zhulong
primary_sources: Records of Penetration into the Mysteries

visual_attributes.morphology: giant dragon with a human face and snake's body
distinctive_features: vertical eyes in a straight seam
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: body is a thousand leagues long
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: human face atop a long, serpentine body

domain_specific_visual.EYE_FUNCTION: creates day and night by opening and closing eyes

relationships.description: Both are serpent-bodied celestial deities in Chinese folklore.
Both are serpent-bodied celestial deities in Chinese folklore.

symbolic_items: herb of penetration into the mysteries

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Han dynasty (3rd century BCE – 3rd century CE)
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: mythical regions beyond the northwest seas
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the power of light and the balance of natural elements

contrastive_constraints: NOT a creature with a purely animalistic face
